statement: Birkhoff-Trjitzinsky predicts the generic leading slope A = 2d for the canonical SIARC PCF recurrence at every d >= 2 from the Newton polygon (slope 2).
evidence: Birkhoff 1930; Birkhoff-Trjitzinsky 1933; Wimp-Zeilberger 1985 sec 3.

statement: In the non-resonant generic case, the SIARC sub-leading parameter alpha is determined by the ratio of the two highest polynomial coefficients of the recurrence; beta = 0 generically.
evidence: standard Newton-polygon analysis (Olver 1974, Immink 1991).

statement: The d = 2 two-branch anomaly is predicted by the discriminant trichotomy Delta_2 = b_1^2 - 4 b_0 b_2: Delta_2 > 0 generic, Delta_2 < 0 oscillatory, Delta_2 = 0 resonance with log n correction (beta = 1).
evidence: Birkhoff-Trjitzinsky discriminant analysis at d = 2; Painlevé classification per H3.

statement: Birkhoff-Trjitzinsky alone does NOT determine the exact sub-leading constants at arithmetic loci (j=0 cubics, j=1728, lemniscatic). The R1.1 j-invariant finer-cubic-split is a residue beyond B-T.
evidence: B-T constructs only formal series; exact constants require monodromy / Stokes data plus modular structure of Lambda(b).

statement: B-T leading-slope prediction A = 2d agrees with Sessions B (50 cubics A=6), Q1 (60/60 quartics A=8), R1.1 (deep cubic ensemble A=6) within fitting noise.
evidence: R1.1 deep-WKB delta cluster around 0; PCF-2 v1.2 Q1 verification table.

statement: The verdict B4_PROVED_AT_d>=3_RESIDUE_AT_d=2 is the correct B-T-only verdict on Conjecture B4: leading slope generically proved at d>=2; d=2 anomaly partially predicted but needs Painlevé corroboration (H3); arithmetic-loci sub-leading is open.
evidence: synthesis of H1-01..H1-05.

statement: Each nonsingular cubic E_b:y^2=b(x) has a canonical SIARC period coordinate tau_b obtained from periods of the invariant differential and reduction to the standard SL2(Z) fundamental domain.
evidence: Standard complex uniformization of elliptic curves; see Silverman Advanced Topics, Ch. I-II, and Diamond-Shurman, Ch. 1.
verdict_dependence: Defines the input variable for all H2 predictions.

statement: For full-level modular forms, Delta=(E4^3-E6^2)/1728=eta^24 and j=E4^3/Delta.
evidence: Diamond-Shurman, A First Course in Modular Forms, Ch. 4 §§4.2,4.4-4.5; Zagier, Elliptic Modular Forms and Their Applications, DOI 10.1007/978-3-540-74119-0_1.
verdict_dependence: Identifies the modular combination behind the empirical log|j| split.

statement: The R1.1 finer split should be attributed to the weight-zero Eisenstein/cusp ratio E4^3/Delta=E4^3/eta^24, not to raw E4 alone.
evidence: j is invariant on X(1), whereas E4 is weight 4; log|j| decomposes as 3 log||E4|| - log||Delta||.
verdict_dependence: Main H2 modular-structure prediction.

statement: j=0 corresponds to the equianharmonic CM point tau=rho where E4 has a simple zero; j=1728 corresponds to tau=i where E6 has a simple zero.
evidence: Classical zero structure of E4 and E6 in the fundamental domain; see Diamond-Shurman Ch. 4 and Zagier 2008.
verdict_dependence: Explains why the observed exact cell is the j=0 cell.

statement: Chowla-Selberg expresses CM eta-values/periods as Gamma-products; at D=-3 the relevant Gamma-values are at thirds and at D=-4 at quarters, up to algebraic and pi factors.
evidence: Chowla-Selberg 1949 open PNAS DOI 10.1073/pnas.35.7.371; Chowla-Selberg 1967 DOI 10.1515/crll.1967.227.86 was paywalled here; supported by accessible secondary formula pages/expositions.
verdict_dependence: Supports the statement that CM Gamma-products affect periods/constants, but source access prevents full theorem certification.

statement: At the equianharmonic j=0 cubic cell, the exact leading coefficient prediction is A_true-2d=0; the Chowla-Selberg Gamma-product is not a nonzero correction to A.
evidence: A nonzero log|j| law would be singular at j=0, while E4(rho)=0 and empirical R1.1/R1.3 show A=6 within tolerance; modular periods affect normalization constants.
verdict_dependence: Directly answers the required closed-form question; confidence limited by inaccessible CM-period sources.

statement: After subtracting the log|j(tau_b)| effect, the next residual discriminator should be the Petersson eta/discriminant height log||Delta||, followed by an E6 or log|j-1728| elliptic-point term.
evidence: log|j|=3 log||E4||-log||Delta||; the remaining natural metric term is eta/Delta, and the next elliptic zero in X(1) is E6 at tau=i.
verdict_dependence: Gives the concrete next T2/T5 regression queue.

statement: Nesterenko's 1996 theorem context supports CM points as exceptional in modular transcendence, but does not itself compute SIARC A.
evidence: MathNet page for Nesterenko, Modular functions and transcendence questions, Sb. Math. 187:9 (1996), 1319-1348, DOI 10.1070/SM1996v187n09ABEH000158.
verdict_dependence: Prevents overclaiming: transcendence literature motivates, not proves, the empirical coefficient.

statement: The requested Yu CM-period/IMRN item was not accessible or bibliographically unambiguous in this run, so no Yu theorem statement is used as evidence.
evidence: OUP/IMRN fetch returned 403 and web-search metadata conflicted between a CM-period item and an elliptic-logarithm item.
verdict_dependence: Forces verdict label LITERATURE_INACCESSIBLE_PARTIAL under the master halt condition.

statement: Sakai's framework classifies discrete Painleve equations as affine-Weyl-group translations on rational surfaces, with the six continuous Painleve equations appearing as degenerations.
evidence: Sakai 2001, Commun. Math. Phys. 220, 165--229, DOI 10.1007/s002200100446; Springer abstract states the rational-surface, affine-Weyl and degeneration construction.
verdict_dependence: Supports requiring a surface/blow-up calculation before declaring a SIARC d=2 discrete Painleve class.

statement: Geometric classifications of discrete Painleve equations use spaces of initial values, Picard lattices, root systems and affine Weyl symmetries; Riccati equations occur as special-function or divisor reductions rather than generic dynamics.
evidence: Kajiwara, Noumi and Yamada 2017, J. Phys. A 50, 073001, DOI 10.1088/1751-8121/50/7/073001; arXiv:1509.08186 abstract describes spaces of initial values, Picard lattices/root systems and affine Weyl groups.
verdict_dependence: Makes the scalar PCF ratio map insufficient for a generic Sakai classification.

statement: The d=2 PCF error recurrence is a rank-2 linear recurrence and its projective ratio is a linearisable non-autonomous Riccati map, not by itself a generic discrete Painleve equation.
evidence: PCF convergent/error recurrence delta_{n+1}=b(n+1)delta_n+a(n+1)delta_{n-1}; ratio y_{n+1}=b(n+1)+a(n+1)/y_n. This algebraic reduction is independent of numerical experiments.
verdict_dependence: Primary reason for verdict D=2_REDUCTION_AMBIGUOUS_NEEDS_NUMERICS rather than confirmed.

statement: If the Delta_2<0 branch admits a nontrivial isomonodromic lift, its most likely continuum Painleve class is P-III(D6).
evidence: The PVI->PV->PIII confluence chain and isomonodromic/Riemann-Hilbert literature (Fokas--Its--Kapaev--Novokshenov 2006; Its--Kapaev 1988) identify PIII(D6) with two non-apparent irregular singular directions. Delta_2<0 suggests oscillatory complex-conjugate transfer multipliers.
verdict_dependence: Would become two-branch confirmation only after singularity and sigma-form tests distinguish it from PV/Riccati.

statement: If the Delta_2>0 branch admits a nontrivial isomonodromic lift, it should be P-V unless an apparent singularity collapses it to a Riccati/Painleve-trivial divisor.
evidence: PV is the first confluence below PVI in the Okamoto/Sakai degeneration chain; real split multipliers are consistent with a surviving regular singular direction, but apparentness must be tested.
verdict_dependence: Explains the proposed Delta_2>0 -> PV or P-trivial branch.

statement: The claim that d>=3 corresponds to a generic P-VI stratum is plausible but not established by the H3 literature pass.
evidence: PVI corresponds to four regular singular directions / D4^(1) surface type in the continuous degeneration guide, but no d>=3 SIARC blow-up or monodromy calculation was available in this theory-only pass.
verdict_dependence: Prevents upgrading the d>=3 statement to a theorem.

statement: The decisive next-session test is geometric, not purely numerical fitting: construct the two-dimensional birational map or Lax deformation, compute blow-up/root type, then check PV/PIII sigma-form residuals under WKB scaling.
evidence: Sakai 2001 and Kajiwara--Noumi--Yamada 2017 make surface/root type the invariant classification datum; Joshi--Kruskal and Kruskal--Joshi--Halburd motivate asymptotic residual tests for Painleve reductions.
verdict_dependence: Operational recipe for resolving D=2_REDUCTION_AMBIGUOUS_NEEDS_NUMERICS.

statement: Ecalle resurgence supplies alien derivatives Delta_omega that encode Borel-plane singularities and the corresponding Stokes jumps.
evidence: Ecalle, Les fonctions resurgentes, PMO 81-05/81-06/85-05; Sauzin, Resurgent functions and splitting problems, Secs. 4-5; Mitschi-Sauzin LNM 2153, Ch. 6.
verdict_dependence: Supports using alien calculus for the zeta_star branch point.

statement: Median summation is the standard ambiguity-cancelling prescription on a Stokes ray, implemented by a half power of the Stokes automorphism.
evidence: Dorigoni, An introduction to resurgence, trans-series and alien calculus, Sec. 6.4; Aniceto-Schiappa, Nonperturbative Ambiguities and the Reality of Resurgent Transseries, arXiv:1308.1115 / CMP 335.
verdict_dependence: Supports median-resurgent treatment rather than one-sided Pade-Laplace summation.

statement: The zeta_star singularity should be modeled as a simple-resurgent algebraic/logarithmic branch point, not as a pole; the exponent should be fitted from large-order data.
evidence: UF-G2-1 established a branch point and Pade logarithmic-rate wall; simple-resurgent local forms in Ecalle/Sauzin/Dorigoni allow algebraic and logarithmic singular germs.
verdict_dependence: Determines the asymptotic extraction ansatz and prevents pole-residue methods.

statement: With A=4/sqrt(3) fixed and 5000 coefficients at 250 dps, direct large-order alien matching should recover the leading Stokes coefficient to at least 30 digits and plausibly 35-45 digits.
evidence: Borel-Darboux/large-order resurgence relates coefficient tails to the nearest Borel singularity; fixing A removes the dominant nonlinear fit parameter. The Pade wall is a branch-cut approximation artifact, not an information limit.
verdict_dependence: Primary basis for verdict MEDIAN_RESURGENCE_GIVES_30+_DIGITS.

statement: A 50-digit extraction is plausible but conditional on absence of a near-degenerate secondary singularity and on correct treatment of logarithmic companions.
evidence: Large-order remainder is controlled by secondary singularities and by unmodeled local terms; these are standard limitations in resurgence extraction workflows.
verdict_dependence: Explains the 30-50 digit range rather than a single guaranteed number.

statement: Closed-form candidates should prioritize Gamma-values at thirds and sixths with powers of pi, 2, and 3; Catalan/Gamma(1/4) is lower priority.
evidence: The exact action 4/sqrt(3) points to Q(sqrt(-3))/equianharmonic geometry; PIII(D6) monodromy constants often reduce to gamma/trigonometric functions under rational monodromy parameters.
verdict_dependence: Closed form is testable but not established, so the verdict should not be MEDIAN_RESURGENCE_GIVES_CLOSED_FORM yet.

statement: Resolving op:cc-branch-resurgence would close the V_quad to P-III(D6) correspondence if the extracted coefficient matches the isomonodromic Stokes/monodromy datum under a documented normalization map.
evidence: Delabaere LNM 2155 demonstrates nonlinear Painleve resurgence/bridge-equation analysis; Garoufalidis-Gu-Marino show Stokes automorphisms can be organized by computable difference/Riemann-Hilbert data in resurgent quantum problems.
verdict_dependence: Supports a Channel Theory theorem-upgrade path, but only conditionally on normalization matching.

statement: Garoufalidis-Marino style resurgence in quantum topology treats factorially divergent perturbative series via Borel singularities, Stokes automorphism matrices, and computable q-difference/Riemann-Hilbert structures.
evidence: Garoufalidis-Gu-Marino, The Resurgent Structure of Quantum Knot Invariants, CMP 386; Garoufalidis-Gu-Marino, Peacock patterns and resurgence in complex Chern-Simons theory, Res. Math. Sci. 10.
verdict_dependence: Provides a modern precedent for turning Stokes data into exact structural correspondence data.

statement: If the proposed extraction gives only about 7-10 stable digits, the likely cause is normalization/model error rather than lack of coefficients.
evidence: The current 7-digit wall comes from global Pade approximation of a branch cut; the direct large-order method uses exact A and thousands of coefficients.
verdict_dependence: Defines a falsification diagnostic for the next T-move.

statement: Complex elliptic curves are classified by period lattices up to SL_2(Z), equivalently by a point tau in H/SL_2(Z) and the modular j-invariant.
evidence: Diamond-Shurman GTM 228 Chs.1-2; Stein Def.1.1 and Thm.1.2 for the modular-group action; LMFDB j-invariant knowl states j=c4^3/Delta for elliptic curves over Q.
verdict_dependence: Supports the existence and canonicity of the E_b -> tau_b map.

statement: For monic SIARC cubics b=x^3+p x^2+q x+r, the recipe j_b=256(p^2-3q)^3/Delta_3 gives the X(1) coordinate of E_b.
evidence: Obtained by translating to depressed Weierstrass form and using the standard j=c4^3/Delta formula documented by LMFDB and Cremona's elliptic-curve invariant algorithms.
verdict_dependence: Enables the 50-row recipe-level tau table.

statement: The points tau=rho=e^{2pi i/3} and tau=i are the elliptic points of the standard fundamental domain, with j=0 and j=1728 respectively.
evidence: Stein Thm.2.11 valence formula singles out i and rho; Silverman Advanced Topics Ch.II treats CM/singular moduli; standard Weierstrass formulas give g2=0 -> j=0 and g3=0 -> j=1728.
verdict_dependence: Identifies equianharmonic and lemniscatic SIARC cells.

statement: The four cubic families with coefficient pattern (1,-3,3,*) in the 50-family catalogue are exactly the j=0 equianharmonic CM subcell rows 30-33.
evidence: For p=-3 and q=3, u=p^2-3q=0, hence j=256u^3/Delta_3=0 for rows 30-33; the empirical prompt reports these four hit A=2d within 1e-3.
verdict_dependence: Shows a genuine CM subcell exists but does not classify all three bins.

statement: The SIARC trichotomy bins are not a CM-locus partition of X(1).
evidence: Only j=0 rows are forced CM; the catalogue bin label -_S3_CM records the imaginary quadratic resolvent of the cubic discriminant, not End(E_b). Most rows in that bin have non-special rational j-values.
verdict_dependence: Rules out TRICHOTOMY_IS_CM_LOCUS_PLUS_RESIDUE as a full trichotomy explanation, except for the isolated j=0 subcell.

statement: The SIARC trichotomy bins are not an X_0(N) partition of X(1).
evidence: X_0(N) parameterizes elliptic curves with a cyclic subgroup of order N; over C every elliptic curve has cyclic order-N subgroups, so the forgetful image in X(1) is not a three-piece partition. No fixed-N isogeny datum is present in the SIARC catalogue.
verdict_dependence: Rules out TRICHOTOMY_IS_X_0(N)_PARTITION.

statement: The observed R1.1 log|j| correlation is best interpreted as a real-analytic drift on X(1), not as a canonical modular-geometric level-set partition.
evidence: The prompt reports Spearman rho(log|j|,A_fit-2d)=-0.691 with Bonferroni p=4e-7, but |j|=c contours are not algebraic modular subvarieties and do not canonically yield exactly three bins.
verdict_dependence: Rules out TRICHOTOMY_IS_J_LEVEL_SET as a literal geometric verdict while preserving j as a useful plotting coordinate.

statement: The d=4 lemniscatic j=1728 cell should be empty in the current PCF/Q1 catalogue window.
evidence: For quartics with j=6912 I^3/(4I^3-J^2), j=1728 is equivalent to J=0, a sparse Diophantine condition; the cubic 50-family catalogue has no irreducible j=1728 row and the prompt reports the lemniscatic cubic cell empty.
verdict_dependence: Provides the requested d=4 lemniscatic prediction under TRICHOTOMY_NOT_MODULAR_GEOMETRIC.

statement: A Klein fundamental-domain X(1) scatter colored by SIARC bin should show an isolated j=0 cluster but no clean three-region geometric separation.
evidence: The 50-row table maps all families to tau_F via inverse j; bin definitions use Galois/resolvent data not visible in complex moduli, so only the CM point should appear geometrically rigid.
verdict_dependence: Recommended falsification/visualization for H5.

statement: The six PCF-1 negative-discriminant non-detections are Q_L01, Q_L02, Q_L06, V_quad, Q_L15, and Q_L26 with discriminants -3, -4, -7, -11, -20, and -28 respectively.
evidence: Local SIARC sources: pcf2_program_statement.tex recap lines 153-164 gives the six Delta_2 values and 18-basis non-detection; channel_theory_outline.tex table lines 567-578 maps the values to family labels.
verdict_dependence: Identifies the roster to which the Chowla-Selberg augmentation is applied.

statement: The Chowla-Selberg formula expresses CM eta-products, and hence CM elliptic-period generators up to algebraic factors, as finite products of Gamma(r/|D|) with exponents determined by the quadratic character chi_D.
evidence: Chowla-Selberg 1967, J. Reine Angew. Math. 227, 86-110, DOI 10.1515/crll.1967.227.86; fetched formula page states both the logarithmic Gamma/eta identity and the individual eta-value product formula.
verdict_dependence: This is the core theorem justifying six Gamma-product basis elements.

statement: A 24-element basis formed by adding one Chowla-Selberg period generator for each of D=-3,-4,-7,-11,-20,-28 is the minimal first augmentation of the PCF-1 18-element basis.
evidence: Each missing family corresponds to a distinct CM discriminant/order, so one period line per order is the dimension-preserving Chowla-Selberg test before splitting into individual Gamma values.
verdict_dependence: Supports the AUGMENTED_BASIS_DETECTS_ALL_6 verdict if PCF limits are elliptic CM periods.

statement: The family Q_L15 at D=-20 is the highest-risk Chowla-Selberg case because the order has class number two, so algebraic class-field multipliers or a root of a class product may be needed in addition to a simple Gamma product.
evidence: D=-20 is the maximal discriminant of Q(sqrt(-5)) with class number two; the Chowla-Selberg class product naturally introduces a root/common-period normalization rather than a single class-number-one eta value.
verdict_dependence: If any family fails B24, Q_L15 is the first place to test algebraic multiplier and normalization variants.

statement: Kontsevich-Zagier periods provide the correct conceptual class for elliptic integrals and CM periods but do not supply a decision procedure or finite PSLQ basis for a PCF limit.
evidence: Kontsevich and Zagier, 'Periods', Mathematics Unlimited--2001 and Beyond, Springer 2001, pp. 771-808, DOI 10.1007/978-3-642-56478-9_39.
verdict_dependence: Prevents upgrading the H6 prediction to a proof without a PCF-to-period map.

statement: Nesterenko's modular-transcendence theorem and Yu's Eisenstein-series transcendence literature support treating CM Gamma-products as genuinely new constants relative to elementary PSLQ bases.
evidence: Nesterenko 1996, Sb. Math. 187:9, 1319-1348, DOI 10.1070/SM1996v187n09ABEH000158; Yu 1989 metadata was partly ambiguous but the cited Eisenstein-series transcendence literature is directly relevant.
verdict_dependence: Explains why the PCF-1 18-element basis can fail without implying absence of closed form.

statement: The 24-element Chowla-Selberg basis is predicted to detect all six PCF-1 missing limits, but the confidence is medium rather than high because PCF-1 has not proven that the limits are pure CM elliptic periods.
evidence: All six discriminants are imaginary-quadratic CM orders covered by Chowla-Selberg; the remaining gap is the unproved PCF-limit-to-elliptic-period identification.
verdict_dependence: Top-line verdict AUGMENTED_BASIS_DETECTS_ALL_6.

statement: Darmon-Vonk and Darmon-Pozzi-Vonk real-multiplication/Borcherds-product work is a fallback theory, not the first-line explanation for these imaginary-CM orders.
evidence: Vonk publication list records Darmon-Vonk, 'Singular moduli for real quadratic fields', Duke Math. J. 170 (2021), DOI 10.1215/00127094-2020-0035; 'Real quadratic Borcherds products', Pure Appl. Math. Q. 18 (2022); and Darmon-Pozzi-Vonk, JEMS 26 (2024), DOI 10.4171/JEMS/1344.
verdict_dependence: If B24 is partial, next augmentation should test Stokes/regulator/RM-type constants rather than more elementary constants.

statement: The cross-hypothesis matrix is consistent and contains no CONFLICT cells.
evidence: AGGREGATOR/cross_hypothesis_matrix.md enumerates all 15 unordered pairs with compatible, independent, or complementary status.

statement: H1 and H2 jointly explain the leading slope plus the j=0 exact cell: B-T gives A=2d and E4(rho)=0 gives A_true-2d=0 at the equianharmonic locus.
evidence: H1 verdict proves the B-T leading slope; H2 verdict gives the provisional Eisenstein-zero mechanism.

statement: H4 and H3 jointly close CC-PIPELINE-G2 UF-G2-1 contingent on matching action, exponent, Stokes coefficient, and median sign to the PIII(D6) normalization.
evidence: H4 supplies branch-germ/Stokes extraction; H3 supplies the conditional Sakai/isomonodromic target.

statement: H5 and H6 jointly provide a modular geometric/arithmetic interpretation but not a stratification theorem.
evidence: H5 rejects a canonical three-stratum geometry on X(1); H6 lists CM Gamma-product constants for arithmetic PSLQ augmentation.

statement: The top-3 T-moves ranked by leverage times tractability are T2, T4, and T1.
evidence: AGGREGATOR/recommended_T_moves.md ranks T2 first for PCF-2 framing, T4 second for V_quad resurgence, and T1 third for B-T theorem closure.
